title: Enigma Stage 4 Postmortem — Production-Patched Schedule & AdamW Mutations
objective: Run mutations directly against production NanoChat MuonAdamW optimizer with all production schedules active. Two attack directions: (A) programmatic schedules on fixed constants, (B) AdamW path mutations (never explored). Baseline = unpatched production.

infrastructure.step_time: ~27ms per step (torch.compile disabled due to triton build failure)

key_methodology_change.description: Stage 4 runs the REAL production optimizer, not the SpecCandidateOptimizer. Mutations are applied as monkey-patches to MuonAdamW. All production schedules are active.

production_schedules.weight_decay: linear decay to 0 over full training

key_methodology_change.previous_eval_gap: Stages 1-3 used cosine LR schedule only. No momentum warmup, no WD decay. H41's +0.032 BPB was partly rediscovering what production already does. Stage 4 eliminates this gap.

stage3_recap.best_mutation: H41 (momentum warmup 0.85→0.95 over 200 steps) at +0.032 BPB
stage3_recap.key_finding: Production NanoChat already has momentum warmup 0.85→0.95 over 300 steps — H41 was rediscovering an existing production schedule
stage3_recap.compounding_lesson: Compounding top mutations hurt: H41 solo (+0.032) > compound H41+H39+H46 (+0.017)

hypothesis_generation.method: 3 parallel subagents with shared context file (enigma/stage4_context.md)
hypothesis_generation.subagent_1: Schedule mutations (H60-H69): 10 hypotheses on fixed constants that could benefit from annealing
hypothesis_generation.subagent_2: AdamW path mutations (H70-H79): 10 hypotheses on the completely unexplored embedding/scalar path
hypothesis_generation.subagent_3: Cross-path interactions (H80-H89): 10 hypotheses on Muon↔AdamW coordination and eval loop corrections
hypothesis_generation.selection_criteria: Coverage across attack surfaces (5 Muon, 4 AdamW), deduplication, low-to-medium risk

portfolio.title: Add trust ratio [0.5,1.5] to production Muon

learning_curves.description: Val BPB at 250-step intervals for top 4 + baseline
learning_curves.note: H64 starts SLOWER (2.011 vs 1.966 at step 250) but crosses over at ~step 3500 and accelerates dramatically in the endgame. Classic 'slow start, fast finish' profile.

H64_nesterov_schedule.mechanism: Decouple Nesterov blend from momentum. Warmup blend from 0.7→0.95 over 500 steps. Less aggressive lookahead early when momentum buffer is poorly initialized.
H64_nesterov_schedule.why_it_works: At initialization, the momentum buffer is zero, so Nesterov lookahead with blend=0.95 means the update is 95% momentum buffer (near zero) + 5% gradient. This is essentially momentum-damped and slow to adapt. With blend=0.7, it's 70% buffer + 30% gradient, giving more weight to the actual gradient signal when it matters most. As the buffer accumulates signal, increasing to 0.95 recovers the full Nesterov advantage.
H64_nesterov_schedule.curve_shape: Starts slower than baseline (noisy early gradients get less smoothing), but the gap narrows rapidly from step 2500 and opens dramatically from step 3500 onward. The endgame acceleration (+0.063 at 5000) suggests the benefit GROWS with training length.
H64_nesterov_schedule.production_implication: 4 lines of code change to production get_muon_momentum: add a parallel get_nesterov_blend function.

H73_eps_schedule.mechanism: Schedule AdamW epsilon from 1e-6 (step 0) to 1e-10 (step 5000) log-linearly. Larger eps early caps per-coordinate step sizes.
H73_eps_schedule.why_it_works: At eps=1e-10, Adam's denominator is effectively 1/sqrt(v). Early in training, v estimates are noisy and some embedding dimensions have near-zero variance, causing enormous effective step sizes. A larger eps (1e-6) acts as an implicit per-coordinate gradient clip. As v estimates stabilize, reducing eps restores full adaptivity. This is particularly important because production uses warmup_ratio=0.0 — there is NO LR warmup, so embeddings see full LR from step 0.
H73_eps_schedule.curve_shape: Consistent advantage throughout. Never slower than baseline at any point. The improvement is steady and compounds over time.
H73_eps_schedule.production_implication: 3 lines in training loop: compute scheduled eps, set group['eps'] for AdamW groups.

H60_beta2_warmup.mechanism: Warm NorMuon second moment beta2 from 0.8→0.95 over 500 steps. Faster variance adaptation early.
H60_beta2_warmup.why_it_works: At beta2=0.95, the variance estimate averages ~20 steps. At initialization when gradient variance is changing rapidly, this is too long a memory — the denominator lags behind the actual variance. Starting at 0.8 (averaging ~5 steps) lets the variance estimate track early changes, preventing under/over-scaling. Converges to production's 0.95 as variance stabilizes.
H60_beta2_warmup.curve_shape: Slightly better than baseline from step 1000 onward, gap widens steadily.

H71_beta1_warmup.mechanism: Warm AdamW beta1 from 0.7→0.8 over 300 steps. Mirrors Muon momentum warmup for AdamW path.
H71_beta1_warmup.why_it_works: Production already warms Muon momentum but leaves AdamW beta1 fixed at 0.8. The same logic applies: lower momentum early means more responsiveness to current gradients when the loss surface is chaotic. This is the AdamW analog of H41.
H71_beta1_warmup.curve_shape: Slow start (lower momentum = noisier early updates) but consistent improvement from step 2000.

H62_wd_cosine.mechanism: Cosine WD decay instead of linear. Keeps WD higher longer, drops faster at end.
H62_wd_cosine.why_it_works: Marginal. Cosine keeps more regularization pressure during the middle of training when the model is still finding structure. The difference is small because WD is already being decayed.

NK17.finding: Trust ratio [0.5,1.5] LOSES on production NanoChat
NK17.explanation: H02's win across Stages 1-3 was an artifact of the broken eval loop that lacked production schedules (no momentum warmup, no WD decay). When these schedules are present, trust ratio is actually harmful. The production optimizer's existing momentum warmup + WD decay already handles the per-layer scale calibration that trust ratio was compensating for.
NK17.implication: ALL Stage 1-3 results based on SpecCandidateOptimizer are unreliable for production decisions. The eval loop gap systematically biased towards mutations that compensated for missing schedules.

NK18.explanation: Despite being standard practice in many frameworks (GPT-3 used wd=0.1), adding even mild WD to embeddings hurts this model. Possible reason: the model uses softcap on logits (tanh squash to ±20), which already implicitly regularizes the output space. Embedding WD fights this by pulling embeddings toward zero while the model is trying to spread them.

NK19.explanation: Overshooting momentum past the target (0.97) then cooling back is worse than the simple linear warmup. The 0.97 peak causes excessive smoothing mid-training when gradients still carry significant new information. Simple schedules beat clever ones.

NK20.finding: Scalar LR warmup is the WORST mutation
NK20.explanation: Delaying updates to resid_lambdas, x0_lambdas, and LayerNorm hurts. These scalars control signal routing through the residual stream. The model needs them to adapt immediately from step 0 to coordinate with the rapidly changing matrix parameters. Production's decision to have warmup_ratio=0.0 (no warmup) is actually optimal for these parameters.

meta_lessons.lesson: Eval loop fidelity is EVERYTHING — running on production eliminates systematic bias
meta_lessons.evidence: H02 trust ratio won all of Stages 1-3 but LOSES on production. H41's +0.032 was partly rediscovering production's existing momentum warmup. Stage 4's production-faithful evaluation reveals completely different winners.

meta_lessons.lesson: Nesterov blend decoupling is the highest-leverage optimizer change discovered
meta_lessons.evidence: H64 (+0.063) is nearly 2x the previous best (H41 +0.032, which was on a biased eval). Decoupling the Nesterov interpolation coefficient from the momentum beta and scheduling it independently is a genuine improvement over production.

meta_lessons.lesson: The AdamW path has massive untapped potential
meta_lessons.evidence: H73 (eps schedule, +0.046) and H71 (beta1 warmup, +0.020) are both AdamW-only changes that beat production. 100% of prior evolution ignored this path. The epsilon dimension was completely unexplored yet delivers the second-best mutation ever.

meta_lessons.lesson: Warmup schedules are the dominant mutation pattern — they work everywhere
meta_lessons.evidence: H64 (Nesterov warmup), H60 (beta2 warmup), H71 (beta1 warmup), H73 (eps warmup/cooldown) — all winners use warmup/schedule patterns. Every fixed constant in the optimizer is a potential warmup target.

meta_lessons.lesson: Simple monotonic schedules beat clever non-monotonic ones
meta_lessons.evidence: H64 (linear warmup 0.7→0.95) beats H63 (overshoot 0.85→0.97→0.95). H60 (linear warmup) beats any complex schedule. The optimizer prefers predictable, smooth transitions.

meta_lessons.lesson: Mutations that appear to work on broken baselines may be harmful on correct baselines
meta_lessons.evidence: Trust ratio (H02/H81) won every stage on the SpecCandidateOptimizer eval loop but LOSES on production. Scalar warmup (H78) seemed reasonable but the -0.011 result shows production's warmup_ratio=0.0 is deliberate, not an oversight.

top_mutations_for_production.title: Nesterov blend schedule 0.7→0.95 over 500 steps
top_mutations_for_production.code_change: Add get_nesterov_blend(it) schedule in base_train.py, apply to Muon groups alongside momentum

top_mutations_for_production.title: AdamW epsilon schedule 1e-6→1e-10 log-linear
top_mutations_for_production.code_change: Compute scheduled eps per step, set group['eps'] for AdamW groups

top_mutations_for_production.title: NorMuon beta2 warmup 0.8→0.95 over 500 steps
top_mutations_for_production.code_change: Add get_beta2(it) schedule in base_train.py, apply to Muon groups

top_mutations_for_production.title: AdamW beta1 warmup 0.7→0.8 over 300 steps
top_mutations_for_production.code_change: Add get_adamw_beta1(it) schedule, apply to AdamW groups

next_steps: Test H64 + H73 compound at 5000 steps (independent paths: Muon Nesterov + AdamW eps, should compound safely)
Test H64 + H60 compound (both Muon schedules, but different parameters)
Test all 4 winners combined (H64+H73+H60+H71) — low risk since they touch 4 different parameters
Test at 10k+ steps to verify H64's endgame acceleration continues
Do NOT deploy trust ratio (H02/H81) to production — it's harmful with correct schedules
Consider making Nesterov blend a first-class tunable in MuonAdamW param groups
